categories.title: Plugin Updates and Improvements in ElizaOS

content.text: The `plugin-browser` has been cleaned up to remove duplicate code following its split from the previously unified `plugin-node`. This refactor helps streamline the plugin's structure and maintainability.

Wallet tests in the `plugin-evm` package have been fixed to improve reliability. This bug fix addresses issues related to test failures and enhances the overall test suite without requiring documentation updates.

The OpenAI plugin now supports custom embedding models. Users can specify `OPENAI_EMBEDDING_MODEL` and `OPENAI_EMBEDDING_DIMENSIONS`, allowing compatibility with alternative OpenAI-compatible providers. The `VECTOR_DIMS` constant was also moved to core for better reuse. Documentation has been updated accordingly.

Improvements were made to the plugin publishing workflow. These include removing forced GitHub pushes, supporting `@elizaos/plugin` prefix aliasing, and refactoring code for better readability and maintainability.
Environment variables for the Raiinmaker plugin were added to the `.env.example` file. This change is non-functional and does not affect runtime behavior or require testing.

An issue was identified in the Twitter plugin where it repeatedly checks the same tweets and mentions, leading to redundant API calls and log spam. Suggested solutions include implementing cursor-based pagination, caching processed tweets, and tracking the last checked tweet ID to reduce system load and improve efficiency.

The current crypto market shows a wide range of asset values. Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) leads with a price of $85,119.08, reflecting its close tie to Bitcoin's value. Wrapped Ethereum (WETH) follows at $1,905.85, maintaining its position as a major Ethereum-based asset. Solana (SOL) is priced at $126.68, continuing to be a significant player in the smart contract platform space. Meanwhile, ai16z, a lower-priced token, is currently valued at $0.1954, indicating its position as a smaller or emerging asset in the market.

categories.title: Refactoring and Fixes for Audio Utilities in ElizaOS

content.text: The ElizaOS project has undergone updates to improve code organization and maintainability related to audio utilities. In one update, repeated audio utility code was moved to the core module to reduce redundancy and centralize functionality.
Another update fixed issues with the starter project and corrected import paths for the relocated audio utilities, ensuring compatibility with the new core structure.

Enhancements have been made to the Telegram integration in Eliza, including the addition of documentation for middlewares and synchronization of `Entity` objects for `message.new_chat_member` and `left_chat_member` events.
A new Telegram community manager feature has been introduced, enabling the bot to greet new users and apply timeouts to users within Telegram chats.

categories.title: Enhancements to Telegram Logic and Development Updates

content.text: The Eliza project introduced enhancements to its Telegram (tg) logic by integrating middleware support, improving the modularity and flexibility of the Telegram bot functionality.

Recent development efforts included package improvements such as a cleanup of the browser plugin, the addition of new features like the enhanced Telegram logic, and the resolution of two bugs. A total of 11 pull requests were merged by 10 contributors, reflecting a collaborative and productive development cycle.

categories.title: ElizaOS Development Updates: Plugin Enhancements, Bug Fixes, and Code Cleanup

content.text: Significant improvements were made to the Telegram plugin, including middleware support, entity synchronization for new and departing chat members, and a new community manager feature that greets and moderates users.
Multiple bug fixes were implemented, including resolving issues with entity synchronization, incorrect payloads in event handlers, and optimizing message handling by removing redundant LLM calls.

Codebase cleanup efforts included moving audio utilities to the core module, removing unused packages, and refactoring the browser plugin to eliminate duplicate code after splitting services into separate plugins.
Several import and configuration fixes were made, including correcting imports for browserify-stream, starter project, and audio utilities.

The OpenAI plugin was enhanced to allow users to specify custom embedding models and dimensions, improving compatibility with OpenAI-compatible providers. The VECTOR_DIMS constant was also moved to the core for reusability.
Plugin publishing workflows were improved by removing forced Git pushes, adding support for plugin name aliasing, and refactoring code for better maintainability.
Wallet tests in the plugin-evm package were fixed to improve reliability, addressing issues from GitHub Issue #397.
Environment configuration was updated by adding Raiinmaker plugin variables to the .env.example file, with no functional changes to the codebase.

A known issue was identified in the Twitter plugin where it repeatedly checks the same tweets, leading to redundant API calls and log spam. Suggestions include implementing cursor-based pagination and caching.
Dependency updates were applied to the dompurify and katex packages, addressing security and performance improvements.
